─[1]-Project──
lorehq
─[2]-Primitives──
Rules Skills Agents Fieldnotes Runbooks
─[3]-Guides──
CommandsCustomizationGuidesPlatform SupportWorking Across Repos
─[4]-Workflow──
Active Work Architecture
─[ Bidirectional Code-Docs Sync ]─ ┐

Bidirectional Code-Docs Sync

Keep the lore source repo and lore-docs public site in sync after changes to either side. This is the operational runbook — for deep accuracy sweeps, use docs-code-alignment-sweep.md.

The Two Repos

RepoPathPurposeContent
lore/home/andrew/Github/loreSource template — what create-lore clonesInstance scaffolding: hooks, scripts, lib, rules, guides for operators, empty knowledge structure
lore-docs/home/andrew/Github/lore-docsPublic documentation site (lorehq.github.io)About Lore: how-it-works, getting-started, cost-evidence, platform guides, hook architecture, examples

These are not mirrors. lore/docs/ is per-instance content (what operators see inside their project). lore-docs/docs/ is about the product (what prospects and users see on the website).

Overlap Points

Topiclore (source)lore-docs (site)Sync direction
Product storyREADME.mddocs/index.mdlore README is canonical, site adapts
First-session guides.lore/templates/seeds/runbooks/first-session/docs/guides/first-session/Seeds are operator-facing; site versions are public-facing rewrites. Update site when seeds change.
Rule contentdocs/context/rules/docs/guides/rules.mdSource rules are the working files; site page describes the system. Update site when rule structure changes.
Hook behavior.lore/hooks/, .lore/lib/docs/guides/platforms/, docs/guides/hook-architecture.mdCode is canonical. After code changes, verify docs match.
Config keys/defaults.lore/config.json, .lore/lib/config.jsdocs/guides/configuration.mdCode is canonical. After adding/changing config keys, update the config guide.
Scripts behavior.lore/scripts/docs/guides/ (various)Code is canonical. After script changes, check if any guide references that behavior.
Platform integration.claude/settings.json, .cursor/, .opencode/docs/guides/platforms/Code is canonical. After platform file changes, update the relevant platform page.
Skill/agent format.lore/skills/, .lore/agents/, .lore/templates/docs/examples/skill.md, agent-rules descriptionsTemplates are canonical. Update examples when format changes.
Release versionpackage.json, .lore/config.jsondocs/changelog.md, version referencesAfter release, update changelog and any version-specific references.

When Code Changes (lore -> lore-docs)

Run after any release or significant code change in the lore source repo.

1. Identify what changed

# From lore repo — changes since last docs sync
cd /home/andrew/Github/lore
git log --oneline --since="YYYY-MM-DD"
git log --since="YYYY-MM-DD" --diff-filter=ACMR --name-only --pretty=format:"" | sort -u

2. Classify changes by doc impact

Change typeDoc action
New hook or hook behavior changeUpdate platform page + hook-architecture
New/changed config keyUpdate configuration.md
New/changed scriptCheck if any guide references it
Rule file added/changedUpdate rules.md if structure changed
Skill/agent template format changeUpdate examples pages
README rewriteReview index.md for alignment
New seed runbookCreate matching first-session guide page
Version bumpUpdate changelog.md

3. Make doc updates

Edit the affected lore-docs pages. Run:

cd /home/andrew/Github/lore-docs
mkdocs build --strict   # catches broken links and nav errors

4. Commit and push

Commit lore-docs with a message referencing the source change:

Update [page] to reflect [lore change]

When Docs Change (lore-docs -> lore)

Run when the public site adds content that should be reflected in the source template.

1. Identify what changed

This direction is less common. It applies when:

  • A getting-started guide reveals a better default for the template
  • A first-session guide improves on its seed runbook
  • The configuration guide documents a behavior that should change in code
  • User feedback on the site reveals a code issue

2. Classify changes

Change typeCode action
Better default discovered while writing docsUpdate template file in lore
First-session guide improvementConsider backporting to seed runbook
Configuration guide reveals missing validationAdd validation to config.js
Doc describes behavior that doesn’t match codeFix the code (code is canonical, but sometimes docs reveal the intended behavior)

3. Make code changes

Edit the lore source. Run tests:

cd /home/andrew/Github/lore
npm test

4. Release if needed

If the code change is user-facing, follow the release process (bump-version.sh + release.sh). Then circle back to the code->docs direction to update the changelog.

After a Release

Every release should trigger a docs check. Minimum:

  1. Changelog — add entry to lore-docs/docs/changelog.md
  2. Version references — grep lore-docs for old version numbers
  3. New features — any new hook, config key, script, or behavior needs a docs update
  4. README — if the lore README changed, review index.md alignment
  5. Build — mkdocs build --strict before pushing

Conflict Resolution

When lore code and lore-docs disagree:

  • Behavioral claims: Code is canonical. Fix the docs.
  • Conceptual framing: Docs are canonical. The site defines how we talk about the product. Code-level naming should align when practical.
  • Examples and snippets: Must match current code. Stale code examples are worse than missing examples.
  • Version-specific content: Always note which version the docs describe. Use the cost-evidence pattern: “Tested on Lore vX.Y.Z” with explicit version callouts.

Verification

After completing a sync in either direction:

# lore-docs: build check
cd /home/andrew/Github/lore-docs && mkdocs build --strict

# lore: test suite
cd /home/andrew/Github/lore && npm test

# Both: check for stale version references
grep -r "v0\.\(OLD_VERSION\)" /home/andrew/Github/lore-docs/docs/

Cadence

  • After every release — mandatory changelog + feature docs
  • After major code changes — hook, config, or platform changes
  • Monthly — quick scan for drift (README vs index.md, config guide vs actual defaults)
  • Quarterly — full docs-code-alignment-sweep.md for deep verification
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
